projects.name: CityHall
projects.summary: Grouped view of the active Aptlantis standards library: WGS, AAMHS, Blanks, CTS, DRS, NeonInk, Blue Slate, PPS, SESM, and WDS. It shows how governance, proposals, releases, command contracts, website deployment, archive integrity, design language, shared UI profiles, and semantic assets fit together.
projects.ecosystemRole: Central standards district for the Aptlantis ecosystem while the framework suite is being phased into the visible portfolio.
capabilities: group active standards under one visible CityHall card
show framework relationships from manifest metadata
surface templates, schemas, examples, validators, and read-first docs
teach which standard applies to proposal, command, desktop, web, archive, visual, and semantic-asset work
keep Blanks visible as a reusable resource kit rather than a governing standard
provide force-directed and Mermaid relationship views for standards adoption
consumes: README, adoption guide, checklist, schema, example, and validator metadata
dependsOn: WGS for workspace placement and City Hall governance
PPS for project intent and proposal intake
NeonInk, Blue Slate, and SESM for visual, UI-profile, and metadata semantics
usedBy: Aptlantis project portfolio
standards adoption planning
new project scaffolding and review
release, command-tool, and website readiness passes
missingPieces: Full SFDS material is referenced as meta_standard but not yet represented as its own visible standards card.
Relationship weights are curated from current manifests and may need maintainer tuning after more standards are phased in.
Cross-project adopter counts are not yet generated from a live scan of every project manifest.
nextSteps: Add adopter counts from the root governance inventory so each standard shows live project coverage.
Promote additional standards into CityHall as they are normalized in D:\.library\aptlantis_core.
Decide the tipping point for splitting standards back into a hidden-folder or separate standards catalog view.
Add per-standard deep links to source docs once public documentation URLs are stable.
potentialImprovements: Generate the relationship graph directly from typed TOML relationship metadata once every standard declares it.
Add graph filters for lifecycle, layer, artifact type, and adopter project class.
Render usage recipes as downloadable project-scaffolding checklists.
Include a live City Hall audit summary from WGS tooling.

screenshots.caption: CityHall visual identity board for the standards hub concept: governance, standardization, guidance, and the WGS/PPS/CTS/DRS framework family. This is brand/explainer evidence, not a release-verification artifact.

projects.helpOutput:
CityHall groups WGS, AAMHS, CTS, DRS, NeonInk, Blue Slate, PPS, SESM, WDS plus the Blanks resource kit.

Resource summary:
- 9 active standards/resources
- 34 template/resource references
- 9 validator entrypoints
- 16 curated framework relationships

Start with WGS for placement, PPS for intent, then route to CTS, DRS, WDS, AAMHS, NeonInk, Blue Slate, or SESM based on the artifact being produced.

resourceKits.title: Blanks
resourceKits.description: Starter manifest and README blanks used when a project, directory, dataset, or standard needs a clean governance shape.

nodes.title: Workspace Governance Standard
nodes.description: Governs the Aptlantis workspace structure, manifests, project registration, lifecycle visibility, services, standards hierarchy, responsibility boundaries, agent orientation, and workspace health.
nodes.scope: Workspace roots, manifests, standards relationships, project classes, lifecycle states, shared services, metadata records, agent startup, and workspace health.

nodes.title: Aptlantis Archive Multi-Hash Standard
nodes.description: Governs archive verification, preservation hash suites, manifest formats, detached signatures, validation procedures, and integrity records.
nodes.scope: Archival integrity, preservation hash manifests, detached signature policy, validation procedures, and archive integrity records.

nodes.title: Command Tool Standard
nodes.description: Governs CLI tools, command contracts, output schemas, exit codes, automation compatibility, and release verification.
nodes.scope: Command-line tools, automation utilities, command contracts, output streams, exit codes, and destructive command safety.

nodes.title: Desktop Application Release Standard
nodes.description: Governs desktop application release notes, manifests, artifacts, hashes, verification, and release evidence.
nodes.scope: Desktop application release readiness and evidence.

nodes.title: NeonInk
nodes.description: Semantic design language for Aptlantis color systems, theme behavior, visual intent, UI language, app surfaces, web surfaces, and generated assets.
nodes.scope: Semantic color systems, visual language, theme contracts, component patterns, desktop app surfaces, web surfaces, and design consistency.

nodes.title: Blue Slate
nodes.description: Shared Aptlantis interface token and starter-pack profile for dark blue-slate application surfaces, including reusable styling resources consumed by desktop and web projects.
nodes.scope: Design tokens, starter-pack resources, app chrome, dark-surface defaults, and reusable implementation guidance for Aptlantis UI work.

nodes.title: Project Proposal Standard
nodes.description: Governs project proposals, design boundaries, success criteria, failure criteria, constraints, risks, roadmaps, and project creation records.
nodes.scope: Project creation, revival, pre-build clarity, proposal readiness, and intent preservation.

nodes.title: SVG Embedded Semantic Metadata
nodes.description: Governs semantic metadata embedded in SVG assets, safe-profile review, privacy/conformance framing, fixtures, and supporting validation/conversion tooling.
nodes.scope: SVG metadata structure, semantic asset records, safe-profile rules, privacy and conformance requirements, validator rules, fixture corpus, conversion support, and agent-readable visual context.

nodes.title: Website Development Standard
nodes.description: Governs websites and web applications as documented, deployable, accessible, metadata-rich, monitorable projects.
nodes.scope: Websites, web applications, deployment records, accessibility, metadata, route checks, rollback expectations, and monitoring.

relationships.label: WGS places proposal-backed work in the drive model
CLI proposals mature into CTS command contracts
Desktop proposals mature into DRS release evidence
Website proposals mature into WDS deployment records
WGS registers command-tool projects and lifecycle state
WGS registers desktop projects and release posture
WGS registers websites and service surfaces
DRS release evidence can use AAMHS archive integrity records
CTS tools that publish archives can use AAMHS manifests
NeonInk gives web surfaces a shared visual language
Blue Slate is a reusable Aptlantis UI profile derived from the visual language layer
Blue Slate starter resources support desktop application surfaces
NeonInk gives desktop releases a shared UI language
SESM gives generated visual assets portable semantic context
WGS keeps semantic asset standards visible in City Hall

usagePatterns.title: Start or revive a project
steps: Write the project proposal so intent, constraints, risks, and success criteria survive the first build.
Place the project through WGS so the directory, local manifest, AGENTS.md, and Project-README.md agree.
Use Blanks as the first pass for the manifest or README when the project does not have a stronger local template yet.

usagePatterns.title: Publish a desktop release
steps: Use DRS for release notes, installer metadata, verification blocks, and evidence folders.
Use AAMHS when the release also needs richer archive manifests, multi-hash records, or detached signature context.
Use NeonInk to keep the user-facing surface consistent with Aptlantis visual language.
Use Blue Slate when a desktop or web surface needs the shared dark application chrome and starter-pack resources.

usagePatterns.title: Ship a command-line tool
steps: Use CTS to define command contracts, output envelopes, exit code bands, examples, and automation-safe behavior.
Use AAMHS when command outputs include archives, bundles, or long-lived preservation artifacts.
Use WGS to register lifecycle state and keep the tool discoverable from City Hall.

usagePatterns.title: Build a web or visual surface
steps: Use WDS for deployment records, accessibility checks, metadata, route checks, rollback posture, and monitoring.
Use NeonInk for palette contracts, component tone, and cross-surface visual consistency.
Use SESM when SVG assets need embedded semantic metadata and safe-profile review.

mermaid.operatingModel:
flowchart LR WGS["WGS
workspace spine"] --> PPS["PPS
intent gate"] PPS --> CTS["CTS
command tools"] PPS --> DRS["DRS
desktop releases"] PPS --> WDS["WDS
web surfaces"] DRS --> AAMHS["AAMHS
integrity records"] CTS --> AAMHS NeonInk["NeonInk
visual language"] --> BlueSlate["Blue Slate
UI profile"] BlueSlate --> DRS NeonInk --> WDS SESM["SESM
semantic SVG metadata"] --> NeonInk SESM --> WDS
mermaid.adoptionFlow:
flowchart TD
    Idea["Project idea or revival"] --> Proposal["PPS proposal"]
    Proposal --> Placement["WGS placement and manifest"]
    Placement --> Class{"Delivery class"}
    Class -->|CLI/tool| CTS["CTS contract"]
    Class -->|Desktop app| DRS["DRS release evidence"]
    Class -->|Website/app| WDS["WDS deployment record"]
    CTS --> Integrity["AAMHS when archives are published"]
    DRS --> Integrity
    WDS --> Visual["NeonInk + SESM when visual assets carry meaning"]
mermaid.artifactChain:
flowchart LR
    Blanks["Blanks starter files"] --> Manifest["Entity manifest"]
    Manifest --> ProjectReadme["Project-README.md"]
    ProjectReadme --> Evidence["Evidence docs"]
    Evidence --> Validators["Validators and checklists"]
    Validators --> CityHall["CityHall overview"]

projects.name: CloneCratesio
projects.summary: Go-based command-tool that mirrors crates.io by expanding a local crates.io-index into downloaded .crate files or rolling tar.zst bundles, emits JSONL manifests and sidecar metadata, exposes Prometheus and pprof endpoints, and provides utilities to generate sidecars and extract bundles. It is operator-focused, local-first, and designed for large-registry, restart-friendly runs and airgap packaging.
projects.ecosystemRole: high-throughput local crates.io mirror and archive pipeline used for offline development, archival capture, and metadata generation
capabilities: mirror crates.io-index to local storage
high-concurrency HTTP/2 downloads with retries
optional rolling tar.zst bundling
emit JSONL per-run manifest audit log
generate per-crate .crate.json sidecars or aggregated JSONL
extract bundles into shard layout
prometheus metrics and pprof endpoints
fast incremental update mode with verify-existing option
wrapper script for index clone/update and downloader invocation
bundle-per-manifest and bundles.index.jsonl
dependsOn: OS filesystem and sufficient disk I/O
usedBy: single operator mirror runs
airgap transport and offline verification workflows
local ecosystem analysis and dataset generation
internal dashboards and Prometheus scraping
downstream forensic/forensic metadata tooling
missingPieces: Formal CTS-style command contracts for each public command (purpose, invocation, inputs, stdout/stderr norms, machine-readable mode, exit codes, examples, stability notes)
Documented exit code table describing mapping to CTS exit code bands
Documented JSON command output envelope (CommandOutput.schema.json) or deliberate justification for per-command JSONL formats
Explicit machine-readable /api/status schema and CLI JSON output schema documentation
Structured error examples and machine-readable error object specification for JSON mode
Release checklist and verified artifact publication steps (build_verified=false, release_verified=false)
CI/workflow for reproducible builds, tests, and release artifact publication (TODOs reference this)
Integration test harness covering end-to-end bundle and extraction workflows (noted in TODO)
Grafana dashboard examples for Prometheus metrics (listed in TODO)
CONTRIBUTING.md, CODEOWNERS, and release automation (not yet created)
nextSteps: Run and record a full build/test/release verification (build binaries, run go test ./..., produce checksums)
Add explicit CTS command contracts per CLI (download-crates, generate-sidecars, extract-bundles, Clone-Index.py)
Document exit code table and map existing os.Exit uses to CTS bands
Publish JSON output schema(s) and adopt a CommandOutput envelope or document rationale for JSONL manifest exception
Create CI workflow to run tests, vet packages, and publish release artifacts with checksums
Author CONTRIBUTING.md and basic release/checklist in repository
Provide Grafana dashboard examples and sample manifests for automation consumers
Reconcile legacy manifest fields and confirm lifecycle state with maintainer (manifest notes request confirmation)
potentialImprovements: Add --json / --format json machine-readable flag with CTS CommandOutput envelope for CLI status endpoints
Provide a formal exit-code mapping doc and ensure all commands return documented codes
Add --dry-run or preview mode to extract-bundles (destructive operation safety)
Publish stable JSON schemas for manifest.jsonl and /api/status and add small JSON schema files into docs/
Add small automation-friendly examples showing how to parse manifest.jsonl and detect errors via exit codes
Ship Grafana dashboard JSON and example Prometheus scrape configs

screenshots.caption: CloneCratesio visual identity board showing the mirror, multiplex, and preserve framing around the crates.io registry mirror. This is concept evidence for project identity, not command-output proof.
Bundle output and manifest evidence from a CloneCratesio run.
CloneCratesio downloader run in progress.

video.title: Architecting an Airgapped Rust Registry
video.description: NotebookLM explainer produced from a complete successful run that downloaded more than 2.5 million files in roughly two hours.

projects.helpOutput:
# Mirror Rust crates from crates.io

## Clone Index

```text
python Clone-Index.py --help

options:
  --index-dir INDEX_DIR
  --output-dir OUTPUT_DIR
  --log-path LOG_PATH
  --threads THREADS                      default: 128
  --include-yanked
  --verify-existing
  --bundle
  --bundle-mode {only,add}               default: only
  --bundle-size-gb BUNDLE_SIZE_GB        default: 8
  --bundles-out BUNDLES_OUT
  --manifest MANIFEST
  --listen LISTEN                        default: :9090
  --progress-interval PROGRESS_INTERVAL  default: 5s
  --progress-every PROGRESS_EVERY
  --dry-run
  --skip-index-update
  --non-interactive, --yes
  --log-level {debug,info,warning,error}
  --downloader-path DOWNLOADER_PATH

legacy compatibility flags:
  --rate-limit
  --resume
  --verify
```

Example:

```powershell
python Clone-Index.py --index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" --output-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io" --threads 128 --progress-interval 5s --non-interactive
```

## Download Crates

```text
go run ./cmd/download-crates -h

important flags:
  -index-dir string
  -out string
  -concurrency int                     default: 128
  -verify-existing
  -bundle
  -bundle-mode string                  only|add, default: only
  -bundle-size-gb int                  default: 8
  -bundles-out string
  -manifest string                     JSONL audit log for the run
  -include-yanked
  -listen string                       default :9090; use :PORT to override or empty string to disable
  -progress-interval duration
  -log-format string                   text|json
  -log-level string                    debug|info|warn|error
```

Loose-file mirror:

```powershell
.\bin\download-crates.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -out "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io" -concurrency 128 -include-yanked
```

Fast update run:

```powershell
.\bin\download-crates.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -out "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io" -concurrency 128
```

Full re-verification of existing files:

```powershell
.\bin\download-crates.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -out "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io" -verify-existing -concurrency 128
```

Bundle-first mirror:

```powershell
.\bin\download-crates.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -out "A:\rust-lang\crates\staging" -bundle -bundle-mode only -bundle-size-gb 5 -bundles-out "A:\rust-lang\bundles" -manifest "A:\rust-lang\manifest.jsonl" -concurrency 128
```

Notes:

- `-bundle-mode only` removes loose `.crate` files after they are added to the archive
- `-bundle-mode add` keeps both loose files and bundles
- bundled runs now produce per-bundle manifests and a top-level `bundles.index.jsonl`
- the downloader prints a cleaner startup configuration block and a final run summary

## Generate Sidecars

```text
go run ./cmd/generate-sidecars -h

important flags:
  -index-dir string
  -out string
  -output-mode string                  files|jsonl, default: files
  -jsonl-out string                    required for jsonl mode
  -manifest string                     optional downloader manifest for bundle metadata
  -concurrency int                     default: 128
  -include-yanked
  -progress-interval duration
  -log-format string                   text|json
  -log-level string                    debug|info|warn|error
```

Loose sidecars:

```powershell
.\bin\generate-sidecars.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -out "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io" -output-mode files -include-yanked -concurrency 128
```

Bundle-oriented JSONL sidecars:

```powershell
.\bin\generate-sidecars.exe -index-dir "A:\rust-lang\crates\crates.io-index" -output-mode jsonl -jsonl-out "A:\rust-lang\bundle-sidecars.jsonl" -manifest "A:\rust-lang\manifest.jsonl" -include-yanked -concurrency 128
```

## Extract Bundles

```text
go run ./cmd/extract-bundles -h

important flags:
  -bundles-dir string
  -pattern string                      default: *.tar.zst
  -out string
  -overwrite
```

Example:

```powershell
.\bin\extract-bundles.exe -bundles-dir "A:\rust-lang\bundles" -out "A:\rust-lang\restored"
```

## Metrics

The downloader exposes metrics on `:9090` by default. Use `-listen :PORT` to override it, or pass an empty string to disable it.

Endpoints:

- `http://localhost:PORT/metrics`
- `http://localhost:PORT/api/status`
- `http://localhost:PORT/debug/pprof/`

projects.name: ChatArchive
projects.summary: Tauri-based local-first desktop app (ChatArchive) that imports OpenAI conversation exports, normalizes conversation trees, extracts artifacts (code, documents, assets, links), stores durable metadata in SQLite, and provides a React reader/dashboard for search, exploration, and export. Rust importer handles sharded OpenAI exports and .dat blobs; frontend is Vite/React; app packaged via Tauri.
projects.ecosystemRole: operator-centric local archive and importer for OpenAI conversation exports; provides normalized archive outputs and artifact indexes consumed by the operator and other Aptlantis data workflows.
capabilities: import OpenAI conversation exports (conversations-*.json and conversations.json)
resolve .dat asset blobs to filenames
normalize conversation trees into ordered threads
index and extract artifacts: code, documents, images, links
persist metadata and viewer state in SQLite (chatarchive.db)
React reader with search, code highlighting, Mermaid/ZenUML rendering
dashboard with archive totals and telemetry
Tauri commands for library setup, import, listing, search, export
produce public/archive-data JSON indexes (index.json, artifacts.json, assets-manifest.json)
interfaces: Tauri IPC / commands (importer, list_code_artifacts, search, export)
Rust backend CLI/commands (src-tauri importer and tests)
SQLite persistence layer accessed by Rust/Tauri
npm scripts / PowerShell QA runners (qa:phase2, test:installer)
produces: normalized per-conversation JSON files in library
packaged Tauri builds (MSI/NSIS produced in QA)
consumes: frontend assets (prism, mermaid-zenuml) and npm package dependencies
dependsOn: rust crates: rusqlite, serde, serde_json, walkdir, regex, chrono
usedBy: herb (maintainer / single operator)
missingPieces: DRS-style release document per-version (release note with theme name, highlights, design boundaries)
Project manifest release fields populated (release.installer.sha256, release.package_version, release.date, release.verified)
Per-version verification block appended to a release checklist (build/tests/install/uninstall/data safety evidence)
Release-evidence bundle (release-evidence/vX.Y.Z/filehash.txt, build.log, test.log, install-check.txt)
Installer lifecycle issue resolution (MSI/installer restore/Windows Installer registration mismatch found in Phase 2 QA)
Recorded signing status for installer artifact (signed / self-signed / unsigned) and signing evidence
Computed SHA-256 of final installer artifact recorded in docs and manifest
Build/test/artifact verification recorded in manifest (manifest.verification flags are false)
Explicit confirmation that docs/ are included in installer/publish output (DRS documentation delivery requirement)
nextSteps: Fix installer lifecycle restore issue identified in docs/Phase2-QA-Report.md (ensure test harness preserves MSI registration or adjust installer strategy) and re-run qa:phase2
Produce a DRS-compliant release document for current candidate (theme name, highlights, design boundaries, built-with, artifact filename)
Build final installer, compute SHA-256 (uppercase hex), record filename and hash in release note and Chat.manifest.toml under release.installer.sha256
Append per-version verification block to the release checklist and update manifest.release.verified with build/test/install/uninstall evidence and test counts
Create a release-evidence folder (build.log, test.log, install-check.txt, filehash.txt) and store alongside docs in publish output
Record installer signing status and, if signing is intended, complete code-signing and include signing evidence in manifest and release-evidence
Include docs/ (release note, trust/security summary, verification summary) in the installer and verify installed copy is accessible to an operator without the source repository
Reconcile manifest.lifecycle/state with the project owner after verification and update lifecycle fields (blocked -> local-verified / published as appropriate)
potentialImprovements: Generate an SBOM (CycloneDX or SPDX) for published installers and store in artifacts/sbom/
Document dependency provenance and exact build flags used for Rust and Node dependencies (templates/Dependency-Provenance.md)
Add a reproducible-build guide (clean-clone build steps with exact tool versions) to reduce future release friction
Automate installer lifecycle QA to preserve and restore Windows Installer registration during native install/uninstall tests
Surface a simple migration contract for chatArchive.viewerState and any future schema changes (templates/Data-Migration-Contract.md)
Provide an authenticated optional export path to package curated code artifacts as a dataset (for upstream Aptlantis generation pipelines)

screenshots.caption: ChatArchive desktop dashboard with the Code Explorer open: imported conversations, visible message counts, unresolved assets, language filters, snippet copy/export controls, and source-conversation navigation.
Document Explorer view that classifies extracted documents and lets an operator inspect standards, plans, notes, and other long-form artifacts without reopening the original chat.
Asset Explorer full-size preview showing copied local assets, source tracing, and visual review of generated or referenced images from imported conversations.
Code Explorer detail view with language grouping, searchable snippets, copy/export actions, syntax highlighting, and a source button for returning to the originating thread.

projects.name: FileCabinet
projects.summary: Local-first Windows desktop vault (VB.NET/WPF) for deterministic retention, cataloging, previewing, integrity verification, repair, and deterministic packaging of high-signal technical artifacts. Includes a CLI, unit tests, WiX installer pipeline, and extensive preservation-focused documentation.
projects.ecosystemRole: Core local-first preservation vault and exemplar desktop release model for the Aptlantis ecosystem; operator-centric archive tool for long-term artifact custody.
capabilities: deterministic ingest (copy/move)
local JSON catalog with extensible hashes
SHA-256 and optional BLAKE3/KangarooTwelve and legacy hash families
vault health analysis and repair preview
thumbnail and extracted-text generation
bulk repair selection and safe apply controls
deterministic packaging/export (folder or zip)
headless CLI for verify, repair, package, rescan, report
WiX MSI installer build pipeline (PowerShell)
release notes and release-verification metadata
consumes: User-selected files and folders for vault ingestion
Existing vault item files during rescan
missingPieces: Documented MSI artifact (FileCabinet-1.7.3.0-win-x64.msi) is not present in the checkout (manifest notes this explicitly).
Local rebuild/verification of the documented v1.7.3 release has not been performed in this checkout (release_verified/build_verified/tests_verified flags false).
Detached signatures (SPHINCS+, PGP) not generated for v1.7.3; release.signatures entries show not generated.
Per-DRS install/uninstall verification evidence (installed window title, uninstall safety checks) not recorded for the current checkout.
Release evidence folder (release-evidence/v1.7.3/) with build.log/test.log/install-check/filehash.txt is not present in the repository snapshot.
nextSteps: Run CI/manual build: dotnet build/test and installer/build-installer.ps1 -Version 1.7.3.0 in a clean clone.
Place resulting MSI under artifacts/installer/ and recompute SHA-256; ensure computed hash matches or update manifest after reconciliation.
Perform install/launch/uninstall checks and record results in manifest.release.verified and append a per-version verification block in the release checklist.
Generate desired detached signatures (SPHINCS+/PGP) or explicitly document unsigned status; record in release.signatures.* and manifest release fields.
Create a release-evidence folder v1.7.3 with build.log, test.log, install-check.txt, uninstall-check.txt, and filehash.txt and commit alongside updated manifest.
If public distribution is intended later, consider producing an SBOM and a reproducible-build guide and update automation scripts to produce them.
potentialImprovements: Automate release-evidence generation and bundling as part of installer/build-installer.ps1.
Add an explicit reproducible-build guide and build flags to make MSI rebuild bit-for-bit when inputs are unchanged.
Produce detached code-signing and PGP signatures for patch/minor releases to strengthen trust evidence.
Add an optional SBOM (CycloneDX) generation step for releases intended for wider distribution.
Tighten verification automation to include install/uninstall smoke tests and record window title in manifest.release.verified.
documentationOutputs: docs/FileCabinet v1.7.3 — Vault Health Workspace Patch.md

screenshots.caption: FileCabinet visual identity board emphasizing retained artifacts, preservation, integrity, and verification. This is project identity evidence; release verification remains governed by the manifest and release-evidence fields.

FileCabinet dashboard showing a live vault with ingest controls, retained size, item counts, recent activity, filtered catalog rows, category navigation, integrity hash status, and vault-health actions.
Vault Health workspace during an Apply Selected Repairs pass, with findings grouped by repairability, selected hash recomputation tasks, progress, and related-item preview context.

projects.name: CodeNote
projects.summary: Narrow Tauri desktop markdown/text editor with a live preview, full PrismJS highlighting, Mermaid diagram rendering, multi-tab editing, explicit file save/open behavior, and an xterm.js-backed native terminal drawer.
projects.ecosystemRole: Small, operator-facing desktop writing and code-note surface that demonstrates the Blue Slate starter-pack contract without adding dashboards, settings screens, cloud sync, or collaboration features.
capabilities: single-window markdown and text editing
CodeMirror 6 editor with language auto-detection
live markdown preview using remark, rehype, and GFM extensions
full vendored PrismJS 1.30.0 highlighting and plugin bundle
Mermaid 11 diagram rendering with a blue-slate theme
multi-tab editing with dirty-state tracking
Tauri open/save dialogs and explicit save-as behavior
xterm.js native terminal drawer
produces: edited markdown, text, and code files saved in place
consumes: local markdown, text, and source files
relative image paths from opened markdown files
usedBy: local Aptlantis note-writing and markdown review workflows
Blue Slate starter-pack validation examples
missingPieces: The main portfolio JSON did not previously contain a review record for CodeNote; this page is constructed from the current README, SPEC.md, package metadata, screenshots, and local manifest path.
The checked-in codenote.manifest.toml currently reads like a schema/template rather than a filled project manifest, so release and governance claims should stay conservative.
No release evidence folder, installer verification, hash record, or public release note is represented in this Studio snapshot.
Build verification is recorded in the CodeNote spec as tsc passing, but the production Vite build was not completed in that source snapshot because of a native Rollup package mismatch.
nextSteps: Replace the schema-like codenote.manifest.toml with a filled project manifest or add a separate manifest instance if the schema file is intentional.
Run and record current build checks from D:\DRS\CodeNote, including TypeScript, Vite production build, and Tauri desktop smoke.
Add DRS release evidence if CodeNote is going to be published as an installable desktop artifact.
Decide whether the Blue Slate starter-pack components that are present but unused should remain as reserved contract examples or move into a separate starter-pack reference.
potentialImprovements: Add screenshot captions tied to specific rendering contracts: markdown, Prism, Mermaid, terminal, and tab lifecycle.
Add a compact trust model explaining local file access, no cloud sync, no autosave, and terminal boundaries.
Record a small smoke test for relative image resolution and external-link handling.
Add release packaging notes once the Tauri bundle path is stable.

screenshots.caption: CodeNote editor and preview surface showing the narrow single-window workflow: source text on one side, rendered markdown/code output on the other, without dashboard navigation.
Rendering evidence for richer markdown output, including syntax-highlighted code and diagram-oriented preview behavior.
Native terminal drawer evidence showing the xterm.js-backed PTY surface docked under the editor workspace.
Tab and workspace evidence showing multi-tab editing, dirty-state handling, and the compact Blue Slate application chrome.

projects.name: CommandWizard
projects.summary: Schema-driven Windows desktop app shipped as Aptlantis Command Wizard. It lets an operator build, edit, and save CLI command workflows through TOML tool schemas, help-text import, guided wizard controls, command previews, history, and favorites.
projects.ecosystemRole: DRS desktop release example and schema-guided command-construction tool; also the origin of the command schema shape imported by AptlantisConsole command-builder workflows.
capabilities: build CLI command strings from TOML tool schemas
edit schemas through a desktop Schema Editor
import schema starters from CLI help text
represent actions, arguments, tags, installed command names, and value styles
guide operators through wizard-based command assembly
save command history and favorites in local AppData
package as a locally signed MSIX
record DRS-style release evidence for build, test, install, uninstall, and hash checks
consumes: CLI help text for schema import
usedBy: single-operator command workflow authoring
AptlantisConsole Terminal -> Command Builder schema parity work
DRS release-evidence examples for desktop packaging
missingPieces: Lifecycle remains marked paused; owner still needs to decide whether the project should be active, archived, or kept as a completed reference tool.
The MSIX is signed with a local developer certificate rather than a publicly trusted code-signing certificate.
Packaging is not byte-for-byte reproducible because each MSIX rebuild receives a different package hash.
The direct makeappx/signtool packaging workaround should be reported upstream to the winapp CLI project if that tool remains in the workflow.
nextSteps: Confirm whether the release-verified v1.0.5 state should move lifecycle from paused to active or archived.
If wider distribution is intended, replace the local developer certificate with a trusted code-signing certificate and update publisher metadata.
Document the one-directional CommandWizard parity relationship now used by AptlantisConsole.
Keep release-evidence/v1.0.5 with README and manifest references synchronized if the MSIX is rebuilt.
potentialImprovements: Add schema export/import examples for common Aptlantis tools.
Record a concise command-schema compatibility note for AptlantisConsole consumers.
Add an optional machine-readable schema validation report before packaging.
Create a public trust note explaining the local-dev signing posture and install requirements.

screenshots.caption: Schema Editor view showing tool records, installed/not-found status, tag badges, schema metadata fields, action controls, and the Arguments grid with Value Style visible.
Wizard view showing schema-guided option selection for Bun, per-option edit controls, and a bottom command preview that assembles the current command string.

projects.helpOutput: Release verification: dotnet build .\CommandWizard.csproj -c Release; dotnet test .\CommandWizard.Tests\CommandWizard.Tests.csproj -c Release (26/26 passed); .\scripts\package-msix.ps1; Get-AuthenticodeSignature .\dist\CommandWizard-1.0.5.0.msix; Add-AppxPackage .\dist\CommandWizard-1.0.5.0.msix.

projects.name: ChromeArchivalPlugin
projects.summary: Chrome MV3 extension packaged as APTlantis Archival. It captures page lists, links, metadata, clean article markdown, full-page markdown, screenshots, and paginated PDFs into deterministic local download folders.
projects.ecosystemRole: Local browser-archival utility for manual web capture and ingestion; produces file artifacts suitable for downstream Aptlantis archive, documentation, and analysis workflows.
capabilities: save active page title, URL, and timestamp to browser local storage
harvest HTTP/HTTPS links from the active page
export saved URL lists as one-link-per-line text files
capture Open Graph fields, JSON-LD, common metadata, and Readability metadata as JSON
export clean article markdown through Readability.js and Turndown
export full-page markdown with absolute links and images
capture viewport and full-page PNG screenshots
export paginated PDFs through Chrome DevTools Page.printToPDF
reject Chrome/system pages where screenshot and PDF capture are not meaningful
interfaces: PowerShell scripts for static validation, packaging, and load smoke
consumes: active browser tabs selected by the operator
page DOM, metadata tags, Open Graph fields, and JSON-LD
Chrome extension APIs: activeTab, scripting, storage, downloads, debugger
usedBy: manual web archival and ingestion workflows
operators preparing pages for Aptlantis archive pipelines
local-first capture workflows that avoid external save-page services
missingPieces: Full interactive capture/export smoke remains manual before public distribution.
The package is an unsigned zip; no signed CRX or Chrome Web Store distribution evidence exists.
Dependency provenance for vendored Readability and Turndown files still needs to be recorded.
Only one curated screenshot is present; popup workflow screenshots still need to be captured.
nextSteps: Repeat interactive capture/export smoke on ordinary HTTP/HTTPS pages before public distribution.
Decide whether the extension remains local-unpacked only or graduates to CRX signing.
Record vendored dependency provenance for Readability and Turndown.
Capture popup screenshots for page list, capture controls, and export state.
potentialImprovements: Add an optional artifact manifest that records every downloaded file path, page URL, timestamp, and capture action.
Add a deterministic sample capture fixture for regression checks.
Expose a compact export summary after each batch capture.
Add a permission rationale panel in the popup or linked trust note for local users.

screenshots.caption: Example metadata JSON produced by the extension, showing canonical URL, Open Graph fields, Readability output, title, URL, and retrieval timestamp opened for inspection.

projects.helpOutput: Release verification: scripts\test-extension.ps1 passed static validation; scripts\package-extension.ps1 produced ChromeArchivalPlugin-v0.1.0.zip; scripts\install-smoke.ps1 launched Chrome with --load-extension for five seconds. Interactive capture/export smoke remains manual.

projects.name: Structra
projects.summary: Tauri desktop application for visual structured-data building. It turns field and object models into exportable JSON and TOML structured data, with the studio route showing a reduced browser-safe version of that core idea.
projects.ecosystemRole: Development tool for local structured data modeling; the studio version demonstrates the same visual-model-to-machine-output concept without presenting workflow automation or schema-authoring as current scope.
capabilities: visual structured data building
nested field and object modeling
live export previews for JSON and TOML
binding-path validation for modeled fields
template support and project save/load flows
structured data validation focused on field paths and example values
interfaces: React desktop UI packaged through Tauri
visual field builder and structure canvas
Tauri Rust backend commands for transform and validation paths
produces: structured data values in JSON and TOML
Tauri desktop application bundles when packaging is run
usedBy: Aptlantis Studio Structra Lab teaching surface
Aptlantis structured-data planning processes
missingPieces: Current build, test, packaging, and release posture were not executed during the 2026-07-08 governance metadata pass.
README still contains stale legacy source-path context and should be reconciled with D:\DRS\Structra.
Release artifact verification is not recorded in the current studio evidence snapshot.
SESM metadata has not yet been embedded in the Structra SVG project mark.
nextSteps: Run and record pnpm build and relevant Tauri checks from the current D:\DRS\Structra source after the scope reduction lands.
Reconcile README and project docs so paths and release posture match the current checkout.
Capture current desktop screenshots for the field builder, export panel, and validation states.
Create SESM-embedded SVG metadata for the Structra project mark when the broader logo metadata pass happens.
Decide whether imports belong in the first reduced desktop scope or should remain deferred.
potentialImprovements: Add automated tests for transformation and validation flows.
Improve packaging and installer automation evidence.
Add import support only after the core JSON/TOML output path is reliable.
Improve accessibility and responsive behavior in the desktop UI.
Add advanced usage notes for structured data modeling once the reduced desktop scope stabilizes.

screenshots.caption: Structra desktop builder view showing the visual structure authoring surface for fields and object paths.
Structure view evidence for how Structra exposes the modeled data shape before export.
Form view evidence showing the same structured model rendered as an operator-editable input surface.
Aptlantis schema view showing Structra output aligned to reusable schema-oriented project records.

projects.name: Logos & Themes
projects.summary: Two-stage Aptlantis asset pipeline: language logo generation and palette-derived editor/terminal themes, exposed through the public Language Atlas picker.
projects.ecosystemRole: Publishes the visual-language layer for Aptlantis programming ecosystem work: generated language logos, palette records, editor themes, terminal themes, and the public Atlas used to inspect and download curated theme selections.
capabilities: catalog programming-language logo identities with labels, descriptions, and public language sites
preserve source SVG logo artifacts and rendered logo families
derive IDE and terminal theme outputs from language palette material
publish searchable public Language Atlas records from copied local artifacts
support mixed language and target selection for browser-generated download packs
keep the held StandardPageGenerator work visible as deferred evidence without reactivating it
expose public-only theme files without regenerating source assets in the browser
record generator verification gaps instead of claiming release-complete theme packaging
interfaces: AptlantisLogos SVG, PNG, ICO, and palette output folders
browser-side ZIP pack generation from public same-origin files
produces: public SVG logo assets copied under /projects/logos-themes/logos
language atlas source catalog copied under /data/language-atlas/source-logos.json
VS Code theme and extension output files
browser-generated ZIP packs for selected language/target combinations
dependsOn: Python generation scripts in AptlantisLogos and LangThemeGenerator
SVG logo assets and palette extraction outputs
theme output conventions for VS Code, JetBrains, Notepad++, terminal, and web targets
React feature route for the Language Atlas browser module
no-dependency stored ZIP helper in the browser
usedBy: Language Atlas public picker
Aptlantis project pages that need language and theme identity assets
theme users downloading editor or terminal packs
future visual-language and design-system documentation
missingPieces: LangThemeGenerator build, test, packaging, and release commands were not re-run during this Studio integration pass.

Theme outputs are copied as current public artifacts; this pass does not regenerate them from source palettes.
StandardPageGenerator remains in holding and is not part of the active public Atlas implementation.
No signed release bundle or external theme marketplace publication evidence is recorded here yet.

Add per-target install notes to the generated ZIP manifest.
